title: Stories about George Morey Miller, chairmen of English Department at University of Idaho
description: George Morey Miller, chairman of the English Department at the U of I, was a combination of "Falstaff, Sam Johnson and Pickwick". His greeting to Mr. Sherman as he joined the faculty. Memories of him remained alive to Moscowans many years after his death. Visiting at the Millers' for the last two Christmas dinners. He called upon Mr. Sherman to take his Victorian class when he became sick.

title: Personal stories about Miller
description: Miller took an interest in everyone. Hunting and his dog Hoosier. Disappearing on a fishing trip.

title: Reading books on Miller's reserve; his assistance to Talbot Jennings, Jennings' work at university
description: Reading the books Miller put on reserve. Miller's passionate interest in folk literature. A student satirist. Miller's recognition and asistance to Talbot Jennings; Jennings' work at the university.

title: Creation of historical pageant "Light of the Mountains"; staging and production
description: Creation of "Light of the Mountains", the university's pageant of Idaho history, conceived by Miller and written by Jennings. Staging and incidents in its production, (continued)

title: Problems staging large-scale pageant
description: Problems of staging a large-scale pageant.

title: Sherman's collaboration on original musical comedies at the university; war recovery of students
description: Mr. Sherman's collaboration on two original musical comedies performed at the university. In G.I.'S Right, their song "Tomorrow and Tomorrow" a slapdash parody, was deeply moving to audiences; it reminded them of life before the war, which they wished to recover. Emotion of students at renewal of fireworks for sports contests after the war.

title: McCarthyism had little effect on campus
description: McCathyism had little effect at the university, although faculty thought it a destructive trend.

title: U of I President Lindley commencement address; commented on lack of communists in Moscow
description: Ernest K. Lindley's commencement address in the thirties; he shocked the audience by stating that it was unfortunate that there were no Communists in Moscow.

title: U of I President Kelly; English Department
description: President Kelly. Role of department chairman. Broad offerings of the English department; English faculty was stimulating.
